nodes.name: Webhook — Receive Excel Upload
nodes.notes: HR team POSTs the daily Excel export here. Accepts multipart/form-data with the file in the 'attendanceFile' field.

nodes.name: Extract Spreadsheet Rows
nodes.notes: Parses the uploaded Excel (.xlsx) file into individual row items. Each row = one punch record.

nodes.name: Aggregate & Transform Punches
nodes.notes: Core transformation: groups all punches per employee per day, derives earliest IN (check-in), latest OUT (check-out), and applies the late rule (>11:00 AM). Also detects missing OUT punches.

nodes.name: Write Daily Records to Google Sheets
Filter — Only Late Arrivals
nodes.notes: Only records where lateFlag = YES flow forward to the warning pipeline.

nodes.name: Update Monthly Late Counter in Sheets

parameters.text:
=You are an empathetic but professional HR communication specialist at Growify.

Draft a warm, personalized first-strike late-arrival warning email for the following employee:

Employee Name: {{ $json.name }}
Employee ID: {{ $json.employeeId }}
Date of Late Arrival: {{ $json.date }}
Check-In Time: {{ $json.checkIn }}
Company Policy: Employees are expected to arrive by 11:00 AM.

Guidelines:
- Tone: Friendly and supportive — this is a gentle reminder, NOT a reprimand.
- Acknowledge that this is the FIRST time (Strike 1 of 3).
- Express understanding that occasional delays happen.
- Clearly state the policy (check-in by 11:00 AM).
- Encourage the employee to reach out to their manager if they're facing any challenges.
- Close warmly.
- Length: 3–4 short paragraphs.
- Do NOT use generic placeholders like [Manager Name]. Address it from "The HR Team at Growify".

Output ONLY the email body (no subject line). Start with 'Dear {{ $json.name }},'.

=You are an HR communication specialist at Growify.

Draft a firm but professional second-strike late-arrival warning email for the following employee:

Employee Name: {{ $json.name }}
Employee ID: {{ $json.employeeId }}
Date of Late Arrival: {{ $json.date }}
Check-In Time: {{ $json.checkIn }}
Total Late Arrivals This Month: {{ $json.newCount }}
Company Policy: Employees are expected to arrive by 11:00 AM.

Guidelines:
- Tone: More serious and direct than the first warning, but still respectful and professional.
- Explicitly note this is the SECOND late arrival this month (Strike 2 of 3).
- Reference that a first reminder was already issued.
- Clearly communicate that a third late arrival will trigger a mandatory meeting with HR.
- Offer support — ask if there is anything causing consistent delays.
- Do NOT use generic placeholders.
- Length: 3–5 paragraphs.

Output ONLY the email body. Start with 'Dear {{ $json.name }},'.

nodes.name: AI — Draft Strike 3 Final Warning Email
Anthropic Claude — LLM
nodes.notes: Shared LLM for all three AI agent nodes via sub-workflow connection.

parameters.start: ={{ (() => { const d = new Date($json.date); d.setHours(17,0,0,0); return d.toISOString(); })() }}
parameters.end: ={{ (() => { const d = new Date($json.date); d.setHours(18,0,0,0); return d.toISOString(); })() }}

nodes.name: Inject Calendar Link into Strike 3 Data

parameters.subject: ={{ `[Friendly Reminder] Late Arrival on ${$json.date} — Growify HR` }}
={{ `[Second Warning] Repeated Late Arrival — ${$json.date} | Growify HR` }}
={{ `[FINAL WARNING + Meeting Required] Late Arrival Policy Breach — ${$json.date}` }}
={{ `[Data Alert] Missing Check-Out Punch — ${$json.date}` }}
parameters.message:
=⚠️ Missing OUT Punch Detected

Employee: {{ $json.name }} ({{ $json.employeeId }})
Date: {{ $json.date }}
Check-In Recorded: {{ $json.checkIn }}
Check-Out: NOT RECORDED

The system could not find an OUT punch for this employee on this date. The daily record has been saved with Check-Out marked as MISSING.

Please verify manually or contact the employee.

— Attendance Automation System

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'success', message: 'Attendance file processed successfully.', processedAt: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

main.node: Write Daily Records to Google Sheets
Update Monthly Late Counter in Sheets
AI — Draft Strike 3 Final Warning Email
Inject Calendar Link into Strike 3 Data

requiredVariables.name: SHEET_ID
requiredVariables.description: Google Sheets document ID containing DailyRecords and MonthlyLateCounter tabs

setupInstructions: 1. Import this JSON into n8n via Workflows → Import from File
2. Add all four credentials listed above under Settings → Credentials
3. Set the SHEET_ID variable under Settings → Variables to your Google Sheet ID
4. Create two tabs in your Google Sheet: 'DailyRecords' and 'MonthlyLateCounter'
5. Activate the workflow — the webhook URL will be displayed in the Webhook node
